unverified.label: Continuous Diffusion of AGI
unverified.quote: This assumption of suddenly having a completely intelligent, fully flexible, fully general human in a box... I don't think that we will have this discrete change. I think we'll arrive at the same kind of gradual diffusion of this across the industry.

unverified.label: Agency Over Intelligence
unverified.quote: Agency > Intelligence I had this intuitively wrong for decades, I think due to a pervasive cultural veneration of intelligence... Agency is significantly more powerful and significantly more scarce.

unverified.label: Build to Understand
unverified.quote: If I can't build it, I don't understand it... don't write blog posts, don't do slides, don't do any of that. Build the code, arrange it, get it to work.

unverified.label: LLMs are Statistical Simulators, Not Magical AIs
unverified.quote: you're not talking to a magical AI you're talking to an average labeler... you're talking to kind of like an instantaneous simulation of that kind of a person that would be hired in the construction of these data sets

unverified.label: Separate Algorithm from Efficiency
unverified.quote: This file is the complete algorithm. Everything else is just efficiency.

unverified.label: Treat the Context Window as a Precious Resource
unverified.quote: think of the tokens in the context window as a precious resource... don't overload it with irrelevant information and keep it as short as you can

unverified.label: The Autonomy Slider
unverified.quote: There's this what I call 'autonomy slider,' where more and more stuff is automated... and we're doing a bit less and less and raising ourselves in the layer of abstraction over the automation.

unverified.label: Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) Tokenization
unverified.quote: we're basically looking for consecutive bytes or symbols that are very common... we're going to group this pair into a new symbol... each time when we mint a new symbol we're decreasing the length and we're increasing the symbol size

unverified.label: Empirical Factuality Tuning
unverified.quote: we interrogate the model three times and we compare its answers... to the correct answer and if the model doesn't know... we create a new conversation in the training set... the answer is I'm sorry I don't know

unverified.label: Few-Shot Prompt Construction
unverified.quote: I used kind of like an XML like language just so that the model understands that the example one begins here and ends here... I just give it a few examples and I kind of like specify them in detail

unverified.label: LLM Pre-training Data Pipeline
unverified.quote: there's a ton of different types of websites that are just eliminated at this stage... the second part is text extraction... The next stage here is language filtering... finishing with for example the pii removal

unverified.label: The LLM Council
unverified.quote: I kind of refer to all these models as my llm Council... If I'm trying to figure out where to go on a vacation I will ask all of them

unverified.label: The Three Stages of LLM Training
unverified.quote: first what we do is we do pre-training... the second thing then is we go into the sft stage... and then finally in the last stage we do all the practice problems in the RL stage

all_results.label: End-to-End Optimization
all_results.quote: Instead of designing and staging explicit algorithms to process images and sentences in complex processing pipelines, our contribution lies in the design of hybrid convolutional and recurrent neural network architectures that connect visual data and natural language utterances with a single network.

all_results.label: Human Cognition ≠ LLM Cognition
